design_principles: Repository interfaces are defined in the domain layer — they are part of the domain model, not application services
Repository implementations are infrastructure adapters — they translate between domain objects and storage technology
No domain logic or invariant enforcement lives in repository implementations
Repository consumers depend only on the interface — never the implementation
Each aggregate has exactly one repository interface; implementations may vary by storage backend
Read-only repositories (User, School, PurchaseHistory) reflect pre-existing fixture data — this system never writes to those tables
Search repositories (Product, ReferralRule) expose only semantic search — never mutation — matching the read-only nature of the vector store at runtime
In-memory implementations are used for all session-scoped aggregates in the prototype — they are architecturally correct, not a shortcut

SQLiteConversationHistoryRepository is the primary implementation for ConversationHistory — it provides durable conversation persistence. InMemoryConversationHistoryRepository is retained as a development/test variant
Persistence mapping is the sole responsibility of the repository implementation — the domain entity is never aware of how it is stored

repositories.name: ConversationSessionRepository
repositories.aggregate_role: aggregate root of BC-1 — full session lifecycle, state machine

methods.purpose: Persist or update the ConversationSession aggregate after state transitions (begin_initialization, complete_initialization, receive_message, complete_turn, close).
Load a session by its unique session_id. Returns None if not found. Used by ProcessTurnUseCase and CloseSessionUseCase.
Return the active or idle session for a user, or None. Used by StartSessionUseCase to enforce INV-CS-7 (one session per user).

implementations.name: InMemoryConversationSessionRepository

persistence_mapping.domain_object: ConversationSession stored by reference — no serialization needed
persistence_mapping.find_active_scan: Linear scan over all dict values; filters on session.user_id == user_id AND session.status in {ACTIVE, IDLE}

implementations.scope: process lifetime — session state is lost on restart
implementations.rationale: Session state is per-turn scoped and does not require durability in the prototype. In-memory provides zero-latency access for the multi-turn pipeline.

invariants_supported: INV-CS-7 — one active/idle session per user enforced via find_active_by_user_id scan
INV-CS-2 — only idle sessions can be closed (enforced by domain; repository persists the result)
repositories.notes: ConversationSession is the primary aggregate root of BC-1. Its lifecycle spans the entire conversation (created → initializing → idle ↔ active → closed). The in-memory implementation is correct for the prototype; a SQLite or Redis-backed implementation would be the natural upgrade path for production.

repositories.name: ConversationHistoryRepository
repositories.aggregate_role: entity — append-only conversation log; two entries per successful turn

methods.purpose: Append a single ConversationHistory entry (user message or assistant response) to the durable log. Called twice per turn by the persist_turn node: once for the user message, once for the assistant response.

Return all ConversationHistory entries for a user ordered chronologically. Used by StartSessionUseCase to load prior conversation context and by ProcessTurnUseCase to detect the prior clarification question.

implementations.name: SQLiteConversationHistoryRepository

persistence_mapping.schema_migration: On __init__, ALTER TABLE adds history_id (TEXT) and session_id (TEXT) columns if not already present. Existing rows receive empty-string defaults — backward compatible with legacy data.

columns.history_id: ConversationHistory.history_id — uuid4 string; 'legacy-{id}' for pre-migration rows
columns.user_id: ConversationHistory.user_id — cast to int for DB write; str on read
columns.session_id: ConversationHistory.session_id — empty string for legacy rows
columns.sender: ConversationHistory.sender.value — translated via _DB_SENDER_MAP before write: 'user'→'user', 'assistant'→'bot' (satisfies CHECK constraint sender IN ('user', 'bot'))

read_mapping.sender: Decoded via _SENDER_MAP: 'user'→SenderType.USER, 'assistant'/'bot'→SenderType.ASSISTANT; defaults to USER for unknown values
read_mapping.timestamp: datetime.fromisoformat() with UTC awareness; defaults to datetime.now(UTC) if parse fails
read_mapping.history_id: Falls back to 'legacy-{row.id}' for rows created before schema migration

implementations.rationale: Durable message persistence. Conversation history must survive session restarts and be queryable for context loading at session initialization.

implementations.name: InMemoryConversationHistoryRepository

persistence_mapping.domain_object: ConversationHistory stored by reference — no serialization

implementations.rationale: Zero-setup alternative for unit testing and local development without a SQLite file. History is lost on restart — not for production use.

operations: find_by_user_id (load full conversation history for history injection into conversation_history: list[dict])

use_cases_served.use_case: UC-2 ProcessTurnUseCase (via persist_turn node in TurnGraph)

invariants_supported: INV-WF-1 — turn persisted to history on successful completion (append called from persist_turn node)
repositories.notes: Conversation history is append-only — there is no update or delete method. ConversationHistory.create() generates a uuid4 history_id per entry. Two entries are appended per successful turn. The full history is loaded in ProcessTurnUseCase via find_by_user_id() and mapped to conversation_history: list[dict] for injection into TurnGraph and all LLM calls (Proposal B — explicit history injection).

repositories.name: UserRepository
repositories.aggregate_role: identity entity — carries user_id, school_id, and name; never mutated by this system

methods.purpose: Load a User identity record by user_id. Returns None if not found. Called by StartSessionUseCase to validate user existence (INV-CS-6) and by ProcessTurnUseCase to load User.name for LLM personalization.

implementations.name: SQLiteUserRepository

persistence_mapping.query: SELECT id, school_id, name FROM Users WHERE id = ?

columns.id: User.user_id — int in DB; str in domain
columns.school_id: User.school_id — int in DB; str in domain
columns.name: User.name — str; used for LLM personalization in ProcessTurnUseCase

persistence_mapping.id_cast: user_id string is cast to int for SQL query; returns None for non-numeric user_ids

implementations.write_operations: none — this system never writes to Users

operations: find_by_id (INV-CS-6 guard — raise NotFoundError if user does not exist)
find_by_id — load User.name for LLM personalization before TurnGraph invocation

invariants_supported: INV-CS-6 — user must exist before a session can be created
repositories.notes: User carries user_id, school_id, and name. name is loaded from the Users.name column and injected into TurnGraphState as user_name. ProcessTurnUseCase uses it alongside school_name to personalize all user-facing LLM responses (generate, generate_clarification, generate_out_of_scope in OpenAIResponseGenerator).

repositories.name: SchoolRepository
repositories.aggregate_role: fundraising anchor entity — provides school_id and name for DecisionContext seeding

methods.purpose: Load the School associated with a user via their school_id foreign key. Returns None if not found. Called by StartSessionUseCase to provide the fundraising school anchor for DecisionContext seeding (INV-CS-3).

implementations.name: SQLiteSchoolRepository

persistence_mapping.join: SELECT s.id, s.name, s.address FROM Schools s JOIN Users u ON u.school_id = s.id WHERE u.id = ?

columns.s.id: School.school_id — int in DB; str in domain
columns.s.name: School.name

persistence_mapping.id_cast: user_id string is cast to int for SQL query

implementations.write_operations: none — this system never writes to Schools

operations: find_by_user_id (raise NotFoundError if school not found; school_name forwarded in PreSeedContextDTO to BC-2)

invariants_supported: INV-CS-3 — school must be pre-seeded into DecisionContext before session becomes active
INV-DC-2 — DecisionContext created with school anchor, not blank
repositories.notes: School is the fundraising anchor of the entire session. Its school_id and name are forwarded to BC-2 via PreSeedContextDTO in StartSessionUseCase. The repository resolves school indirectly through the user's school_id foreign key — there is no direct school_id lookup from the application layer.

repositories.name: PurchaseHistoryRepository
repositories.aggregate: PurchaseHistory (raw dict records — intentionally no domain entity wrapper)
repositories.aggregate_role: purchase signal source — provides pre-session purchase data as preference seeds for BC-2

methods.purpose: Return all purchase history records for a user as raw dicts. Returns empty list gracefully when no records exist. Used by StartSessionUseCase to build the purchase_signals list for PreSeedContextDTO sent to BC-2.

implementations.name: SQLitePurchaseHistoryRepository

persistence_mapping.join: SELECT ph.id, ph.product_id, p.name AS product_name, ph.quantity, ph.purchased_at FROM Purchase_History ph JOIN Products p ON p.id = ph.product_id WHERE ph.user_id = ? ORDER BY ph.purchased_at DESC
persistence_mapping.domain_entity: none — raw dict by design; purchase records are signal seeds for BC-2, not first-class domain entities in BC-1

implementations.write_operations: none — this system never writes to Purchase_History

operations: find_by_user_id (optional signal — empty list handled gracefully; dicts forwarded in PreSeedContextDTO.purchase_signals to BC-2 PreSeedContextUseCase)

repositories.notes: PurchaseHistory intentionally returns list[dict] rather than a domain entity. This is a deliberate design decision: purchase records are used only as preference signal seeds to bootstrap DecisionContext in BC-2. They cross the BC-1→BC-2 boundary as primitive data in PreSeedContextDTO. There is no purchase domain entity in BC-1 and adding one would create unnecessary coupling.

repositories.name: DecisionContextRepository
repositories.aggregate_role: aggregate root of BC-2 — stateful preference signal accumulator scoped to a session

methods.purpose: Persist or update the DecisionContext aggregate. Called by PreSeedContextUseCase at session initialization, and by ProcessTurnIntelligenceUseCase after each turn (post-graph action).
Load the DecisionContext for the given session. Called at the start of each turn by ProcessTurnIntelligenceUseCase before invoking the DecisionIntelligenceSubgraph.

implementations.name: InMemoryDecisionContextRepository

persistence_mapping.domain_object: DecisionContext stored by reference — no serialization needed
persistence_mapping.scope: one entry per active session; entries are never explicitly deleted in the prototype

implementations.scope: process lifetime — DecisionContext is lost on restart
implementations.rationale: DecisionContext accumulates preference signals within a session. Session state is inherently transient in the prototype — cross-restart persistence is not required. In-memory ensures zero-latency access across all turns. The natural upgrade path for production is SQLite serialization or Redis.

operations: save (create and persist initial DecisionContext with school anchor and purchase signals)
save (persist updated context after graph returns — post-graph action)

invariants_supported: INV-DC-2 — DecisionContext created with school anchor (enforced by domain; repository persists the result)
INV-DC-5 — signals accumulate monotonically (enforced by context.add_signal(); repository persists accumulated state)
INV-CS-5 — DecisionContext seeded at session initialization (save called by PreSeedContextUseCase)
repositories.notes: DecisionContext is the stateful core of BC-2. Its signal list grows monotonically across turns — the repository simply overwrites the previous snapshot on each save (last-write-wins for the session-scoped key). The context's status transitions (empty→partial→ready→enriched) are all derived from domain methods; the repository stores and returns the current state without interpreting it.

repositories.name: ProductRepository
repositories.aggregate_role: read-only domain entity — carries product facts retrieved from vector store

methods.purpose: Execute a semantic similarity search over the product_catalog Qdrant collection. Returns the top_k most relevant Product entities ranked by cosine similarity to the query embedding. Called by ExecuteRetrievalUseCase when source_target == 'product_catalog'.

implementations.name: QdrantProductRepository
constructor_params: qdrant_url: str — Qdrant server URL (e.g. http://localhost:6333 or Qdrant Cloud URL)
openai_client: OpenAI — shared OpenAI client for embedding generation
qdrant_api_key: str | None = None — API key for Qdrant Cloud authentication; None for local instances

persistence_mapping.embedding_model: text-embedding-3-small — same model used during ingestion; guarantees query-document vector space alignment

payload_to_domain.product_id: payload.product_id OR payload.doc_id OR str(point.id) — fallback chain
payload_to_domain.name: payload.name
payload_to_domain.attributes: {} — empty dict; not stored in current ingestion schema

implementations.write_operations: none — runtime read-only. Write side: QdrantVectorStoreWriter (ADAPT-1) — offline ingestion pipeline.

invariants_supported: INV-AR-2 — only retrieved products reach the response generator; no hallucination path
INV-WF-3 — retrieval completes before evidence is passed upstream to BC-1 for generation
repositories.notes: Product retrieval is purely semantic — no filtering by price, category, band, or stock is implemented at this scope. The same OpenAI embedding model used during ingestion (text-embedding-3-small) is used at query time to guarantee correct cosine distance comparisons. Embedding is generated inside the repository on every search call.

repositories.name: ReferralRuleRepository
repositories.aggregate_role: read-only domain entity — carries referral program rule facts retrieved from vector store

methods.purpose: Execute a semantic similarity search over the referral_program_rules Qdrant collection. Returns the top_k most relevant ReferralRule entities ranked by cosine similarity. Called by ExecuteRetrievalUseCase when source_target == 'referral_program_rules'.

implementations.name: QdrantReferralRuleRepository
constructor_params: qdrant_url: str — Qdrant server URL (e.g. http://localhost:6333 or Qdrant Cloud URL)
openai_client: OpenAI — shared OpenAI client for embedding generation
qdrant_api_key: str | None = None — API key for Qdrant Cloud authentication; None for local instances

persistence_mapping.embedding_model: text-embedding-3-small — same model used during ingestion

payload_to_domain.title: payload.label
payload_to_domain.rule_type: Mapped from payload.label via _LABEL_TO_RULE_TYPE: 'eligibility'→ELIGIBILITY, 'bonus'→BONUS, 'invitation'→INVITATION, 'requirement'→REQUIREMENT; defaults to ELIGIBILITY for unknown labels

implementations.write_operations: none — runtime read-only. Write side: QdrantVectorStoreWriter (ADAPT-1) — offline ingestion pipeline.

invariants_supported: INV-AR-3 — only retrieved referral rules reach the response generator; no hallucination path
INV-WF-3 — retrieval completes before evidence is passed upstream to BC-1 for generation
repositories.notes: The label→RuleType mapping (_LABEL_TO_RULE_TYPE) is the only domain-level translation beyond field assignment. Defaults to ELIGIBILITY for unrecognized labels — ensures the entity is always valid even if the Qdrant payload carries an unexpected label value from the ingestion pipeline.

aggregate_repository.description: Full read/write lifecycle for a mutable aggregate root. Methods: save (create or update) + find_by_id / find_by_session_id.

append_repository.description: Append-only write + chronological query read. The entity log grows over time; individual entries are never updated or deleted.

read_only_repository.description: Query only — this system never writes to these tables. Reflects pre-existing fixture data managed externally.

search_repository.description: Semantic similarity search only. No CRUD. Qdrant collections populated by the offline ingestion pipeline, not by this system's runtime.

Conversation_History.migration: history_id and session_id columns added on first use

qdrant.embedding_model: text-embedding-3-small — shared between write (ingestion) and read (runtime) sides

in_memory.note: Python dicts for session-scoped aggregate state that is transient by design in the prototype
repositories: REPO-2 defaultdict(list) keyed by user_id — dev/test variant only
